summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 10 warning, 12 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: рџџЎ bin/lib/gate.mjs:266 вЂ” `internalError` catch block falls back to exit-code verdict, which can produce false PASS when structured-output processing throws. Safer default would be FAIL on internal error.
рџџЎ bin/lib/gate.mjs:199-211 + bin/lib/run.mjs:87-98 вЂ” Config-loading + outputFile-reading duplicated ~10 lines between `cmdGate` and `runGateInline`. Extract a `buildConfigOverride()` helper.
рџџЎ bin/lib/gate.mjs:118 + bin/lib/run.mjs:56 вЂ” Path-traversal sanitization regex duplicated identically. Extract to shared `isUnsafeTaskId()`.
- Confirmed prior review's two рџ”ґ dead-code findings (`isGenericJson`, dead `verdict` variable) are both fixed
рџџЎ test/parsers.test.mjs:757 вЂ” `harnessJSON` helper duplicated at line 1382; extract to module scope

рџџЎ bin/lib/gate.mjs:56 вЂ” `config?.text || combinedOutput` uses `||` instead of `??`. Empty output file (`""`) silently falls back to stdout parsing instead of producing 0 findings. No test covers this. Same pattern at run.mjs:99.

рџџЎ `bin/lib/parsers.mjs:75` вЂ” `extractAttr` interpolates `name` directly into `new RegExp()` without escaping. Currently safe (hardcoded callers only) but violates defense-in-depth. Escape regex metacharacters or annotate as `@internal`.

рџџЎ `bin/lib/gate.mjs:207` + `bin/lib/run.mjs:94` вЂ” `validators.json` `outputFile` is not sanitized for `..` path traversal. Same trust boundary as package.json scripts, but inconsistent with the `taskId` sanitization at gate.mjs:118.

рџџЎ `bin/lib/gate.mjs:266-268` вЂ” `internalError` catch fallback uses `exitCode === 0 ? "PASS" : "FAIL"`, which could produce a false PASS if internal processing throws after format detection. No test covers this catch path.
- рџџЎ `test/parsers.test.mjs:757,1382` вЂ” `harnessJSON` helper duplicated verbatim (24 lines) across two describe blocks. Hoist to module scope.
- рџџЎ `test/parsers.test.mjs:782,1407` вЂ” `setupGateFeature`/`setupFeature` near-identical setup helpers. Merge into one.
